title: Probabilistic Sketches Structure
description: JSON structure documentation for probabilistic data structures (sketches) used in streaming analytics and approximate query processing

HyperLogLog.description: Cardinality estimation sketch for counting distinct values with ~2% error using O(log log n) space
HyperLogLog.useCase: COUNT DISTINCT queries on large datasets, unique visitor counting, distinct IP tracking

precision.description: Precision bits controlling accuracy vs memory tradeoff

CountMinSketch.description: Frequency estimation sketch that approximates the frequency of elements in a data stream
CountMinSketch.useCase: Heavy hitter detection, network traffic analysis, trending topics, frequency queries

width.description: Number of columns (controls error rate = e/width)

depth.description: Number of hash functions/rows (controls failure probability)

errorRate.description: Maximum overcount per query = epsilon × total_count

confidence.description: Probability that estimate is within error bound

BloomFilter.description: Probabilistic membership testing structure that answers 'might be in set' or 'definitely not in set'
BloomFilter.useCase: Cache invalidation, URL deduplication, spell checkers, database query optimization

capacity.description: Expected number of elements to insert

bitArraySize.description: Size of the bit array in bits

itemCount.description: Number of items currently in the filter

TDigest.description: Quantile estimation sketch for computing percentiles, medians, and trimmed means over streaming data
TDigest.useCase: P95/P99 latency tracking, histogram approximation, percentile analytics, SLA monitoring

compression.description: Compression factor controlling accuracy (higher = more accurate)

quantile.description: The quantile to estimate (e.g., 0.99 for P99)

estimate.description: Estimated value at the queried quantile

count.description: Total count of items added to the sketch

ThetaSketch.description: Set operations sketch from Apache DataSketches enabling union, intersection, and difference on estimated sets
ThetaSketch.useCase: Audience overlap analysis, A/B group comparisons, multi-dimensional cardinality, funnel analysis

k.description: Nominal entries parameter controlling accuracy (power of 2)

isEmpty.description: Whether the sketch contains no data

tradeoffs.memoryVsAccuracy: All sketches trade memory for accuracy. Higher memory configuration yields lower error rates.
tradeoffs.exactVsApproximate: Exact algorithms (e.g., exact COUNT DISTINCT) require memory proportional to data size. Sketches use fixed memory with bounded error.
tradeoffs.updateVsQuery: Sketches are optimized for high-throughput updates; some (like T-Digest) have O(log n) query time.
tradeoffs.mergeable: Most sketches are mergeable: two sketches of the same type can be combined into one, enabling distributed computation.
